metadata.title: E2E Test - First-Time User Assessment Journey

story.iWant: comprehensive E2E test covering the full assessment flow
story.soThat: I can confidently deploy knowing the onboarding experience works end-to-end
story.tasks:
1. Navigate to `/assessment` 2. Verify wizard renders with "Question 1 of 10" 3. Answer all 10 questions (using test data for consistent results) 4. Verify progress bar reaches 100% 5. Verify "Next" button changes to "Finish" on Q10 6. Click "Finish" 7. Wait for ResultsSummary to render 8. Verify all 3 domain cards present 9. Verify scores displayed correctly (based on test answers) 10. Verify "Start Training" button present 11. Click "Start Training" 12. Verify navigation to `/training`
Additional Requirements: - Test runs in mobile viewport (375×667) - Test takes screenshots at: Q1, Q5, Q10, Results - Test verifies IndexedDB contains assessment record - Test completes in <30 seconds - Test passes on Chromium, Firefox, WebKit

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: **Given** all assessment stories are complete (Story 2.6 done) **When** I run the E2E test suite **Then** a Playwright test exists (`tests/e2e/assessment-flow.spec.ts`) that executes all test steps successfully

doc.title: Discalculas Architecture Specification
doc.snippet: E2E Tests: Playwright 1.56.1 with cross-browser support. Mobile-first viewport (375×667). Tests located in tests/e2e/. 100% test coverage mandatory for all code.

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: 10-question wizard covering number_sense (Q1-4), spatial (Q5-7), operations (Q8-10). Final step displays ResultsSummary with domain scores, navigates to training with weights pre-loaded. All data persisted to IndexedDB.

doc.title: Architecture Specification
doc.snippet: Tests located in tests/e2e/. Use Playwright with accessible selectors (getByRole, getByLabel). Screenshots on failure. Trace on first retry.

artifact.reason: Reference pattern for Playwright tests in this project - shows standard structure, mobile viewport usage, and test organization
Main assessment route component that orchestrates the wizard flow, calculates scores on completion, and displays ResultsSummary. Primary target for E2E test.

Results screen component that displays domain scores with color-coded cards (coral/yellow/mint), confetti animation, completion time, and "Start Training" button. Test must verify this renders correctly.
First question type in assessment (Q1-2). Test needs to interact with this component.
Second question type (Q3-4). Test interacts with number line slider.
Spatial awareness question (Q5-6). Test must answer rotation questions.
Spatial pattern question (Q7). Test selects matching pattern.
Math operations questions (Q8-9). Test inputs numeric answers.
Word problem question (Q10). Final question before results.
Scoring algorithm that calculates domain scores from answers. Used by AssessmentRoute to generate results.
Saves assessment results to IndexedDB (Dexie). Test should verify storage with page.evaluate().
Playwright configuration defining mobile viewport (375×667), browser projects (chromium/firefox/webkit), and test settings (30s timeout, screenshots on failure).

dependency.purpose: E2E testing framework with cross-browser support
Unit/integration test runner (not used for E2E)
IndexedDB wrapper - test must verify assessment saved to DiscalculasDB

constraints.constraint: Must use Playwright's accessible selectors (getByRole, getByLabel, getByText) per architecture standards
Test must run in mobile viewport 375×667 (iPhone SE) as per project configuration
Test timeout: Maximum 30 seconds total (playwright.config.ts line 18)
Must clear IndexedDB before test: await page.evaluate(() => indexedDB.deleteDatabase('DiscalculasDB'))
Test must pass on all three browser engines: Chromium, Firefox, WebKit
Screenshots required at: Q1, Q5, Q10, Results page (stored in test-results/assessment-flow/)
Mock performance.now() for consistent timing in test data
Use test data that produces deterministic, expected scores for verification
100% coverage required - this E2E test completes the assessment epic coverage

interface.name: /training route
interface.signature: GET /training → Target navigation after completing assessment

interface.name: ResultsSummary Props
interface.signature: { domainScores: DomainScores; completionTime: { minutes: number; seconds: number }; onStartTraining: () => void }

interface.name: DiscalculasDB.assessments table
interface.signature: { id: string; timestamp: string; domainScores: DomainScores; totalQuestions: number; correctAnswers: number }

tests.standards: Playwright E2E tests with cross-browser support (Chromium, Firefox, WebKit). Mobile-first viewport (375×667 iPhone SE). Use accessible selectors: page.getByRole(), page.getByLabel(), page.getByText(). AAA pattern: Arrange, Act, Assert with clear comments. Screenshots on test milestones and failures. Tests located in tests/e2e/ directory. Run via: npm run test:e2e CI integration via GitHub Actions (already configured).

ideas.idea: Create tests/e2e/assessment-flow.spec.ts that: 1. Clears IndexedDB before test 2. Navigates to /assessment 3. Verifies wizard renders with "Question 1 of 10" 4. Takes screenshot at Q1 5. Answers all 10 questions using test data (deterministic answers for reproducible scores) 6. Verifies progress bar updates (10%, 20%, ..., 100%) 7. Takes screenshot at Q5 (mid-point) 8. Takes screenshot at Q10 (before completion) 9. Verifies "Next" button text changes to "Complete" on Q10 10. Clicks "Complete" button 11. Waits for ResultsSummary to render (with loading state) 12. Takes screenshot of results page 13. Verifies 3 domain cards are present 14. Verifies domain scores match expected values based on test answers 15. Verifies completion time is displayed 16. Verifies "Start Training" button exists 17. Verifies IndexedDB contains assessment record: page.evaluate(async () => { const db = await Dexie.open('DiscalculasDB'); return db.assessments.toArray(); }) 18. Clicks "Start Training" 19. Verifies navigation to /training route 20. Confirms test completes in <30s
Test implicitly uses mobile viewport (375×667) as configured in playwright.config.ts for all projects
Playwright projects in config run test automatically on chromium, firefox, webkit
